publicFacts.claim: Kaleidos is described publicly as a transportable high-temperature gas-cooled microreactor using TRISO fuel, helium coolant, and prismatic graphite moderator blocks.
publicFacts.sourceTitle: NRC advanced reactor pre-application page: Kaleidos
publicFacts.boundary: Public architectural description only; no proprietary design basis inferred.

publicFacts.claim: Radiant markets Kaleidos as a portable microreactor intended to replace similar-sized diesel generators for remote sites, defense installations, data centers, and critical assets.
publicFacts.boundary: Marketing-level mission statement; not a verified deployment claim.

publicFacts.claim: Radiant has publicly discussed an R-50 production facility and related licensing path for manufacturing microreactor units.
publicFacts.sourceTitle: Radiant R-50 factory site license announcement
publicFacts.boundary: Used as a toy readiness milestone, not as a licensing conclusion.

publicFacts.claim: Radiant has publicly tied Kaleidos demonstration work to the National Reactor Innovation Center DOME test path at Idaho National Laboratory.
publicFacts.boundary: Public program milestone only; no safety significance implied.

publicFacts.claim: Radiant has publicly discussed Buckley Space Force Base as a target context for microreactor deployment under the Advanced Nuclear Power for Installations program.
publicFacts.boundary: Scenario context for synthetic readiness analysis; not a deployment guarantee.

milestones.title: Public pre-application engagement
milestones.note: Source-linked fact set establishes what the demo is allowed to say.

milestones.title: DOME demonstration readiness
milestones.note: Synthetic compute bundle mirrors how evidence is gathered and triaged.

milestones.title: R-50 manufacturing path
milestones.note: Requirements matrix emphasizes configuration control and objective evidence.

milestones.title: Installation-facing deployment scenario
milestones.note: Fleet monitoring and site-readiness checks stay synthetic and public-safe.

requirements.text: The console shall distinguish public facts from synthetic analysis outputs at every user-facing claim boundary.
requirements.rationale: Interview demo must communicate disciplined technical claims without implying proprietary or safety-significant analysis.

requirements.text: The workbench shall provide a deterministic transport-style toy calculation with reproducible scalar flux, leakage, and balance metrics.
requirements.rationale: Computational transport background should show up in a concrete, safe, small calculation.

requirements.text: The readiness bundle shall include passive thermal margin and load-following toy checks linked to test-readiness evidence.
requirements.rationale: Kaleidos public positioning emphasizes portable power and air-cooling context; the demo needs an engineering-facing thermal thread.

requirements.text: The project shall maintain a requirements-to-evidence matrix with artifact hashes and controlled status.
requirements.rationale: The hidden signal is mature engineering evidence practice without making compliance claims.

requirements.text: The scheduler emulator shall expose queued, running, completed, failed, and held states with traceable logs.
requirements.rationale: The role calls for HPC workload scheduling, job failure analysis, and stakeholder triage.

requirements.text: The diagnostic engine shall map environment failures to a root cause, next action, and preventative deployment control.
requirements.rationale: The demo should show Linux/HPC debugging rather than just charts.

requirements.text: The frontend shall display source-linked public facts, compute jobs, evidence packs, and deployment checks from controlled fixtures.
requirements.rationale: A compact app should still preserve traceability and fixture validation.

requirements.text: The DevOps layer shall define dry-run-safe local container, Terraform, and Ansible artifacts for hybrid compute readiness.
requirements.rationale: The role asks for Linux, file systems, process management, networking, Ansible/Terraform-style infrastructure, and resilient compute environments.

requirements.text: The project shall maintain controlled quality, design, verification, and release documentation for the v2 and v2.1 baselines.
requirements.rationale: Reviewers should be able to inspect the engineering-control story without relying on oral explanation.

requirements.text: The release process shall provide version-aware checkpoint, branch-fold, and release-hygiene scripts with dry-run capability.
requirements.rationale: Baseline transitions should be reproducible and recoverable without cloning release logic for every version.

requirements.text: The documentation quality check shall verify required controlled document structure, metadata, and v2.1 traceability content.
requirements.rationale: Release readiness needs enforceable documentation completeness checks.

requirements.text: The release operation scripts shall support version-aware dry-run execution, signed checkpoints, conservative exclusions, and v2 compatibility wrappers.
requirements.rationale: Release operations should be previewable before mutating git state and should not depend on version-specific script copies.

requirements.text: The backend gateway shall preserve public-safe default behavior while providing an opt-in integration path for real Slurm submission.
requirements.rationale: The v3.0 backend needs real handler structure without implying access to Radiant infrastructure.

requirements.text: The Slurm gateway shall require authorized mTLS client identity for submit and status handlers.
requirements.rationale: Backend calls should have a clear trust boundary and no browser-held private keys.

requirements.text: The Slurm gateway shall expose health, readiness, submit, job-status, and Prometheus-format metrics handlers.
requirements.rationale: Operations need a minimal handler surface before other backend processes are added.

requirements.text: The Slurm gateway shall support mock mode by default and opt-in sbatch mode through validated configuration.
requirements.rationale: The demo must remain deterministic while preserving a real integration seam.

computeJobs.title: One-dimensional SN transport toy sweep
logs: balance residual converged below synthetic threshold

computeJobs.title: Passive air-cooling margin toy check
logs: peak cladding proxy remains below synthetic limit

computeJobs.title: Fleet telemetry anomaly pass
logs: checking drift, packet loss, and thermal trend outliers
one pressure channel flagged for engineering review

computeJobs.title: Held transport rerun: worker module mismatch
logs: scheduler: job failed before restart file open
triage: worker image is stale relative to scheduler module index

diagnosis.rootCause: Worker image drift: worker-b lacks the transport module advertised by the scheduler.
diagnosis.nextAction: Drain worker-b, rerun Ansible baseline, refresh module index, then resubmit from the last verified artifact.
diagnosis.preventativeControl: Add CI check that compares scheduler module inventory with worker role templates before deployment.

evidencePacks.title: Transport toy sweep objective evidence
evidencePacks.summary: Deterministic SN-style toy sweep completed with bounded balance residual and traceable artifact manifest.
evidencePacks.limitations: Synthetic calculation only; not a reactor physics, shielding, criticality, or safety analysis.

evidencePacks.title: DOME readiness thermal toy evidence
evidencePacks.summary: Passive cooling toy margin remained positive under the synthetic ambient and heat-load case.
evidencePacks.limitations: Synthetic lumped educational model; not qualified thermal hydraulics, accident analysis, or licensing evidence.

evidencePacks.title: Fleet telemetry toy anomaly evidence
evidencePacks.summary: Synthetic fleet screen found one channel requiring engineering review while packet loss remained below the demo threshold.
evidencePacks.limitations: Synthetic telemetry only; not a remote operations, cybersecurity, or plant monitoring qualification.

evidencePacks.title: HPC failure triage objective evidence
evidencePacks.summary: Failed rerun was diagnosed as worker image drift before restart file access, with an Ansible baseline action and deployment gate.
evidencePacks.limitations: Synthetic scheduler and module logs; not evidence of any actual Radiant infrastructure.

controlledEvidence.title: Controlled documentation baseline
controlledEvidence.summary: Controlled quality, design, verification, release, and requirements records are maintained as fixture-linked evidence.
controlledEvidence.limitations: Controlled documentation package only; not a formal compliance, licensing, or production quality-system claim.

controlledEvidence.title: Version-aware release tooling baseline
controlledEvidence.summary: Generic release scripts provide version-aware checkpoint, fold, and cleanup flows while v2 wrappers preserve existing commands.
controlledEvidence.limitations: Controlled local release tooling only; dry-run checks do not create remote refs or mutate GitHub state.

controlledEvidence.title: Mock-first Slurm gateway backend baseline
controlledEvidence.summary: The v3.0 Go gateway provides core backend handlers, mTLS identity enforcement, request validation, deterministic mock spooling, opt-in sbatch spooling, and Prometheus-format counters.
controlledEvidence.limitations: Controlled mock-first gateway only; default mode is synthetic and does not prove access to Radiant or production Slurm infrastructure.

deploymentChecks.finding: Scheduler module index is controlled, but artifact mount latency is above the synthetic readiness threshold.
Worker baseline requires module inventory refresh before transport restart jobs are allowed.
Artifact path, retention policy, and manifest hash checks are aligned with the objective evidence index.
Monitoring endpoint accepts worker health checks; alert route remains in dry-run mode.
